task_completion.description: Verify that a clinical encounter or evaluation is opened for the patient John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A GNU Health window showing a patient evaluation, encounter, or health record screen with 'John Zenon' selected as the patient.

task_completion.description: Verify that the diagnosis of Legionnaires' disease (ICD-10: A48.1) is recorded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The conditions, diseases, or diagnosis section of the evaluation shows 'Legionnaires' disease' or the ICD-10 code 'A48.1'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a high fever is documented in the clinical evaluation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The vital signs or clinical evaluation section shows a temperature reading of 39.0 (Celsius) or higher.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least two diagnostic laboratory tests are ordered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The lab tests, investigations, or orders section shows at least two distinct laboratory tests requested for the patient.

task_completion.description: Verify that a targeted antibiotic is prescribed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The medication, prescriptions, or treatment section shows an order for Azithromycin, Levofloxacin, or Ciprofloxacin.

task_completion.description: Verify that a follow-up appointment is scheduled for 3 to 7 days in the future.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The appointments section or a calendar view shows a new appointment scheduled for John Zenon with a date that is 3 to 7 days after the current system date.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GNU Health GUI client.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the graphical user interface of GNU Health being clicked and typed into, rather than terminal windows executing SQL commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface must be the genuine GNU Health application, not a text editor, notes app, or mock-up where the agent simply typed out the required information.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the application UI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no open terminal windows showing 'psql' or python scripts being used to directly insert records into the GNU Health database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient: John Zenon. Required ICD-10 code: A48.1. High fever must be documented as a temperature >= 39.0 C. At least 2 lab tests must be ordered. Prescribed antibiotic must be exactly one of: Azithromycin, Levofloxacin, or Ciprofloxacin. Follow-up appointment must be scheduled between 3 and 7 days from the current date.
